metadata.title: Quantum revival: the shape that falls apart and reassembles
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Quantum Mechanics Vol. 5 candidate 05. Source: M-05 (Fourier Series), parabolic state in the square well, quantum revival.
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL=coherent shape (revival — constructive interference); CRIMSON=dephased scrambled density (chaos between revivals)
metadata.source: Vol. 5, M-05 (Fourier Series and the Wave Equation), parabolic state in the square well
metadata.note: Exclusions: do not derive Fourier coefficients; collapse-and-return is the payoff.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B12: palette=claude but the outro is 'OutroCTA' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Scramble a wavefunction into noise. Wait. Watch it rebuild itself — exactly — from nothing but time.

card.dek: Quantum revival: the shape that falls apart and reassembles

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. Scramble a wavefunction into noise. Wait. Watch it rebuild itself — exactly — from nothing but time.

beats.narration_text: An initial wavefunction spreads into an incoherent smear. The modes are spinning at different rates. There is no restoring force. So why does the original shape come back?

card.dek: Modes dephase freely — no restoring force. Yet the original shape returns. Why?

build.suggested: suggested prompt: An initial wavefunction spreads into an incoherent smear. The modes are spinning at different rates. There is no restoring force. So why does the original shape

beats.narration_text: A parabolic wavefunction in a box is a sum of standing-wave modes. Each mode spins its phase at a rate proportional to n squared — faster and faster for higher modes.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A parabolic wavefunction in a box is a sum of standing-wave modes. Each mode spins its phase at a rate proportional to n squared — faster and faster for higher

beats.narration_text: At first the modes walk in step. Then they drift: the fast ones race ahead, the slow ones lag behind. The probability density churns into an incoherent smear.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: At first the modes walk in step. Then they drift: the fast ones race ahead, the slow ones lag behind. The probability density churns into an incoherent smear.

beats.narration_text: The key: the mode frequencies are n-squared times a base rate. All ratios of those frequencies are rational numbers. That means the clocks are commensurable — they will all realign.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The key: the mode frequencies are n-squared times a base rate. All ratios of those frequencies are rational numbers. That means the clocks are commensurable — t

beats.narration_text: At the revival time, every mode has completed an integer multiple of its cycle. They all return to exactly the phase they had at the start. The original shape snaps back.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: At the revival time, every mode has completed an integer multiple of its cycle. They all return to exactly the phase they had at the start. The original shape s

beats.narration_text: The revival is not oscillation — it is constructive interference of many clocks all ticking at integer multiples of a base frequency. The box enforces commensurability.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The revival is not oscillation — it is constructive interference of many clocks all ticking at integer multiples of a base frequency. The box enforces commensur

beats.narration_text: Quantum revivals have been observed in Rydberg atoms and in Bose-Einstein condensates. They are a signature of a discrete, commensurable spectrum.

props.headline: Quantum revivals have been observed in Rydberg atoms and in Bose-Einstein…

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Quantum revivals have been observed in Rydberg atoms and in Bose-Einstein condensates. They are a signature of a discrete, commensurable spectrum.

beats.narration_text: Illustrative: particle in a box, parabolic initial state. Mode 1 frequency = 1 unit. Mode 2 = 4 units. Mode 3 = 9 units. Revival time: when all modes return to integer multiples. At t = 1 all are back. Parabola reformed.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Illustrative: particle in a box, parabolic initial state. Mode 1 frequency = 1 unit. Mode 2 = 4 units. Mode 3 = 9 units. Revival time: when all modes return to

beats.narration_text: Modes dephase at n-squared rates. All ratios rational — clocks commensurable. At the revival time every clock completes an integer cycle. The shape returns.

card.dek: Commensurable n^2 frequencies -> revival. Dephase, then rephase. Constructive interference of clocks.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Modes dephase at n-squared rates. All ratios rational — clocks commensurable. At the revival time every clock completes an integer cycle. The shape returns.
